Came to Natal. disembarked at Durban. Feb. 6. 1903, f admitted as an cated entrant. From 1903. to 1909" resided in Ladysmith. During this period | devoted spare time in acquaint- ing leading Indians ‘overseas of the position of Natal Indians, par. ticularly the plight of labourers on the rail ways and the coal mines of Northern Natal. Special correspondent to the late Dadabhai Naroojce, late Sir M. M. Bhownagree, late Sir Henry Cotton, MP. and Me, M. Ke candin. When he eft Ladysmith was publicly thanked by Railway Indian empl for services rendered. After a short stay in India returned in 1908. At this stage he separated from Mr. Gandhi on the question of passive resistance, inclining to the view of constitutional approach. From 1910 became a resident of the Transvaal, stayed at Klerks- dorp up to 19: in interested himself with public work, being responsible for the large public demon- stration accorded to the late Hon. G. K. Gokhale, when he visited Klerksdorp. Since coming to Lichtenburg was responsible for starting the Government Indian School. Publications : “History of the Deboo y for the last Four Hundred Years” and news- Articles, being correspondent of “Jama Jamshed. logy. Address: 224, Market St., Johannesburg, Transvaal. DEENDAR, Hajee Ismail Mahomed ; 6. Kholvad dist. Surat, 1877; educ. Kholvad. Was one of the Pioncer inhabitants of Kholvad to come to South Africa in 1889, settling first in Johannesburg. From there left for Heidelburg, returned to India. and when came back stayed at Ermelo. Started own business as general dealer, adding several branches in the district. Went to Standerton in 1902 and in 1916 transferred interests to Breyten, where he is at present, being the proprietor of the firm of Amod and Ismail,” Treyten and Estantia, the leading Indian firm in this area. 1s also owner of the Breyten Trading Co., and sole proprietor of the Breyten Building (Pty.), Ltd. In 1930 went on pilgrimage, with to Mecca. Was associated with 3 Gandhi in passive resistance movement he serving a term of imprisonment Apart from taking actual part in the struggl provided food for the strikers at Standerton spending in that way much money as well. [n India was trustee of the Kholvad Juma (old mosque). In the discharge of his public duty, he had earned the respect of the entire community. Is a committee member of the Kholvad water works and Madressa Anjuman Islam Kholvad. Keenly interested in education, has sons at Aligarh Muslim University and Kathor High School, and has provided several bursaries for indigent children.
